data.description:
## Why

Attestation manifests must carry the rebar gate-code version+SHA for audit
provenance (epic jira-reb-596). This story adds the value where it is signed and
the parser to read it back ‚Äî the core of the feature, with no build or display
surface yet.

## What

- Add a gate-code SHA/version resolver in `src/rebar/signing.py` (co-located with
  and reusing the `head_sha()` idiom, line 337 ‚Äî a `git -C ‚Ä¶ rev-parse` subprocess;
  do NOT invent a new git wrapper). It returns `"<version> (<short-sha>[-dirty])"`,
  or just `"<version>"` when no SHA is resolvable:
  - Version: `rebar.__version__`.
  - SHA: `git -C <dir of rebar.__file__> rev-parse --short HEAD` ‚Äî the **rebar
    source (gate code)** dir, NOT the target repo. Resolution is **live-git-first,
    omit-last** (build-baked fallback = story 2).
  - `-dirty` rule: append `-dirty` iff `git -C <dir of rebar.__file__> status
    --porcelain` is non-empty (any staged / modified-tracked / untracked change in
    the rebar source tree).
  - Any git failure -> omit the SHA (version only) and emit a debug log
    (silent-failure guard).
- Append one `rebar-version:` line to BOTH manifests:
  - `build_manifest` ‚Äî `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` (plan-review).
  - `_verdict_manifest` ‚Äî `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`
    (completion-verifier).
- Add `manifest_rebar_version(manifest)` in `attest.py` (mirroring
  `manifest_regver`) returning the stamp, or `None` for pre-stamp manifests.
- `compute_validity` stays untouched and must never read the stamp.

## Scope

Signing + parsing only. Build-time baking = story 2; `show`/`verify_signature`
surfacing = story 3.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `build_manifest` and `_verdict_manifest` both emit a `rebar-version:` line.
- [ ] Resolver returns live-git short SHA with `-dirty` per the rule above, and
      omits the SHA (version only, + debug log) on git failure / non-git install.
- [ ] `manifest_rebar_version()` round-trips the stamp and returns `None` for a
      pre-stamp manifest.
- [ ] `compute_validity` behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged; a pre-stamp
      manifest still validates.
- [ ] Unit tests: both manifests carry the line; live-checkout resolution incl.
      `-dirty`; git-absent omit; parser round-trip + pre-stamp `None`; no
      validity impact.
data.title: Stamp gate-code version+SHA into attestation manifests + parser
